projects.title: AI News Feed — March 14–21, 2026
projects.description: A curated, thematic AI news feed tracking the voices, signals, and narratives that matter for AI-powered software engineering. Organized around recurring themes rather than raw chronology. Continues from [the March 8–13 edition](https://github.com/AgentiaPT/agentia-research/blob/main/projects/9995-2026-03-13-ai-news-feed/README.md).

projects.title: The Civilization That Forgot How to Code
projects.description: Around 370 BCE, Socrates made an argument against writing. In Plato's *Phaedrus*, he warned that the written word would ["create forgetfulness in the learners' souls"](https://fs.blog/an-old-argument-against-writing/#:~:text=create%20forgetfulness%20in%20the%20learners%27%20souls) — that people who relied on text would "appear as though they were all-knowing, but to actually be learners of nothing." They would become hearers of many things but knowers of nothing.

projects.title: The Gutenberg Parallel: Software Democratization and the Coming Volume Explosion
projects.description: What is happening now with software happened before with writing. The pattern is unmistakable: a skill once reserved for a trained elite is being democratized by a new technology, and the volume of output is about to explode in ways that are hard to imagine.

projects.title: The Great Budget Reallocation: From SaaS Seats and Headcount to Token Budgets

explorers.title: Token Budget Calculator
explorers.description: Interactive calculator modeling enterprise budget shifts from SaaS seats and headcount to AI token budgets

projects.title: Git Secret Detection & Sensitive Information Leak Prevention
projects.description: Comprehensive landscape analysis of tools, practices, and patterns for preventing secrets and sensitive data from leaking into git repositories. Special attention to AI-authored content risks and PII detection beyond traditional API key scanning.

projects.title: Hardening the Claude Code Sandbox: A Practical Security Guide
projects.description: Claude Code ships with a sandbox that provides OS-level process isolation for all Bash commands. On Linux and WSL2 this uses [bubblewrap (bwrap)](https://github.com/containers/bubblewrap), creating isolated namespaces for PID, network, mount, IPC, UTS, and cgroup. On macOS it uses Apple's native Seatbelt framework.

projects.title: Can LLMs Self-Improve Code Without Feedback? An Empirical Test
projects.description: An empirical experiment testing whether LLMs can genuinely improve code through iterative self-reflection — or whether "self-correction" is just resampling in disguise.

projects.title: AI News Feed — March 8–13, 2026
projects.description: A curated, thematic AI news feed tracking the voices, signals, and narratives that matter for AI-powered software engineering. Organized around recurring themes rather than raw chronology.

explorers.title: AI News Feed Dashboard
explorers.description: Visual dashboard with voice position maps, signal radar, theme coverage, and key quotes

projects.title: Iterative Self-Improvement at Inference Time vs. Gradient Descent During Training
projects.description: When an LLM generates an output, critiques it, and refines it iteratively, is this process formally analogous to gradient descent on a loss function? The answer is: partially yes, with important caveats.

projects.title: Inference-Time Compute Scaling in Large Language Models
projects.description: Inference-time compute scaling (also called "test-time compute" or "test-time scaling") refers to the practice of allocating additional computational resources *during inference* -- when a model is generating its response -- rather than during training. Instead of making a model smarter by training it longer on more data (training-time scaling), you make it smarter by letting it "think" longer on each problem.

projects.title: Why LLMs Improve When They Critique and Refine Their Own Outputs
projects.description: A deep research survey on self-refinement, self-correction, and iterative improvement in large language models.

explorers.title: The Paradox of Self-Improvement
explorers.description: Interactive research essay on why LLMs produce better output when forced to critique and refine their own work

projects.title: CLAUDE.md Token Budget Analyzer
projects.description: How much of Claude's 200K context window do `CLAUDE.md` and config files consume before the conversation even starts?

explorers.title: Token Budget Dashboard
explorers.description: Visual breakdown of CLAUDE.md token consumption with per-file usage, context window budget, and optimization tips
